original_image_tags: Grayscale sensor still, likely infrared (IR) or low-light television, showing a wide field of view. A white crosshair reticle with graduated tick marks is centered. The horizontal axis is labeled with numbers: 15, 10, 5, [center], 5, 10, 15. The vertical axis has unlabeled tick marks. A small, dark, dot-like object is visible just above the horizontal axis and slightly to the right of the vertical axis (approximately at coordinates [2, 1] relative to the center crosshair). The background is a uniform gray with visible sensor noise. The bottom of the frame shows a hazy, lighter-toned horizon line. Multiple black rectangular redaction blocks obscure text or data fields across the image: one large block across the top, and three pairs of smaller blocks on the left and right sides, positioned above and below the horizontal axis. A timestamp is overlaid in white digital font in the bottom left corner.

elements.description: A grayscale image displaying a centered crosshair reticle with horizontal tick marks labeled 5, 10, and 15 on both the left and right sides. A small, dark circular object is visible just above the horizontal axis near the center. A timestamp '12/31/99 18:11:34' is visible in the bottom-left corner. There are seven solid black rectangular redaction blocks obscuring portions of the image: one wide block across the top, and three on each side.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual elements (grayscale image, crosshair reticle, dark object, timestamp location, black redaction blocks), but Description B quotes a specific timestamp string ('12/31/99 18:11:34') that Description A omits, and Description A provides more detail about the crosshair labels and object position without contradicting B.
